section_name: Implementing MOUD: Pain Management, Opioid Use, and Addiction in Alaska
instructors: Dustin Muse, Bekah Meyer, Aaron Walbrecher, Muse, Dustin; Meyer, Bekah; Walbrecher, Aaron
public_notes: This self-paced online training equips healthcare providers with comprehensive, evidence-based knowledge and skills to deliver compassionate, inclusive care for individuals with Opioid Use Disorder (OUD) while effectively managing related pain. The course covers opioid pharmacology, pain management principles, Medications for Opioid Use Disorder (MOUD), ethical care, stigma reduction, and coordinated recovery support through interactive lessons featuring clinical scenarios, knowledge checks, and reflective prompts.

description: Part 1 of 3 with a focus on MOUD

section_name: Provider Clinical Support System & Training Portal
course_name: Provider Clinical Support System & Training Portal
course_display_name: Provider Clinical Support System & Training Portal
description:
*****Advertising Only: You will need to register with the vendor. See below for more information******

For more information and to register, visit the Provider Clinical Support System website.


Provider Clinical Support System (PCSS) is a national training and clinical mentoring project developed in response to the opioid use disorder crisis. Our education and training resources were developed for primary care providers. The overarching goal of PCSS is to provide the most effective evidenced-based clinical practices in the prevention of OUD through proper opioid prescribing practices, identifying patients with OUD, and the treatment of opioid use disorder. Make sure you filter course options to display only those that provide CME/CE credit.

Highlighted trainings on this site include:
  • Chronic Pain Core Curriculum
Advertising information: this is a clearinghouse of continuing education opportunities, some of these are linked to outside vendors. This requires you to register on their site. Some events come with a fee and that must be paid to the vendor. For more information on accessing external courses through this clearinghouse contact uaa_akcache@alaska.edu
